prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_shipment pickup_vehicle_1)
(assign_vehicle_asset_to_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 vehicle_asset_a)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(mark_shipment_ready pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(unassign_transport_mode_from_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(stage_cargo_at_origin pickup_vehicle_1 origin_hub_a cargo_pallet_1)
(register_shipment delivery_vehicle_1)
(assign_vehicle_asset_to_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 vehicle_asset_b)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(mark_shipment_ready delivery_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(allocate_loading_dock_for_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 loading_dock_1)
(reserve_destination_node_for_delivery delivery_vehicle_1 destination_hub_b mode_road)
(confirm_delivery_preparation delivery_vehicle_1 destination_hub_b loading_dock_1)
(release_loading_dock_from_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 loading_dock_1)
(unassign_transport_mode_from_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(create_transport_leg_with_origin_staged pickup_vehicle_1 delivery_vehicle_1 origin_hub_a destination_hub_b leg_1)
(finalize_transport_leg_readiness leg_1 pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(finalize_origin_staging pickup_vehicle_1 origin_hub_a mode_road cargo_pallet_1)
(unassign_transport_mode_from_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(register_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1)
(assign_vehicle_asset_to_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1 vehicle_asset_c)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1 mode_road)
(mark_shipment_ready vehicle_unit_truck_1 mode_road)
(stage_container_for_leg vehicle_unit_truck_1 container_1 leg_1)
(reserve_service_window_for_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 service_window_morning)
(apply_service_window_and_stage_vehicle vehicle_unit_truck_1 container_1 leg_1 mode_road service_window_morning)
(assign_handling_equipment_to_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 forklift_1)
(allocate_loading_dock_for_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1 loading_dock_1)
(assign_handling_equipment_and_prepare_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 forklift_1 loading_dock_1 container_1 leg_1)
(assign_driver_to_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 driver_1)
(assign_driver_and_prepare_vehicle_with_load_profile vehicle_unit_truck_1 driver_1 container_1 leg_1)
(assign_load_profile_to_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 load_profile_standard)
(sequence_loading_and_commit_to_vehicle vehicle_unit_truck_1 pickup_vehicle_1 delivery_vehicle_1 mode_road load_profile_standard)
(finalize_vehicle_dispatch_flag_after_loading vehicle_unit_truck_1)
(assign_time_window_to_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1 time_window_slot_1 mode_road)
(release_loading_dock_from_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1 loading_dock_1)
(unassign_handling_equipment_from_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 forklift_1)
(unassign_driver_from_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_1 driver_1)
(unassign_transport_mode_from_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_1 mode_road)
(reconcile_and_release_vehicle_unit_asset vehicle_unit_truck_1 vehicle_asset_c time_window_slot_1)
(register_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2)
(assign_vehicle_asset_to_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2 vehicle_asset_c)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2 mode_road)
(mark_shipment_ready vehicle_unit_truck_2 mode_road)
(stage_container_for_leg vehicle_unit_truck_2 container_2 leg_1)
(reserve_service_window_for_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 service_window_afternoon)
(apply_service_window_and_stage_vehicle vehicle_unit_truck_2 container_2 leg_1 mode_road service_window_afternoon)
(assign_handling_equipment_to_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 forklift_1)
(allocate_loading_dock_for_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2 loading_dock_1)
(assign_handling_equipment_and_prepare_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 forklift_1 loading_dock_1 container_2 leg_1)
(assign_driver_to_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 driver_1)
(assign_driver_and_prepare_vehicle_with_load_profile vehicle_unit_truck_2 driver_1 container_2 leg_1)
(assign_load_profile_to_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 load_profile_fragile)
(sequence_loading_and_commit_to_vehicle vehicle_unit_truck_2 pickup_vehicle_1 delivery_vehicle_1 mode_road load_profile_fragile)
(finalize_vehicle_dispatch_flag_after_loading vehicle_unit_truck_2)
(assign_time_window_to_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2 time_window_slot_1 mode_road)
(release_loading_dock_from_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2 loading_dock_1)
(unassign_handling_equipment_from_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 forklift_1)
(unassign_driver_from_vehicle_unit vehicle_unit_truck_2 driver_1)
(unassign_transport_mode_from_shipment vehicle_unit_truck_2 mode_road)
(reconcile_and_release_vehicle_unit_asset vehicle_unit_truck_2 vehicle_asset_c time_window_slot_1)
(activate_pickup_vehicle_on_leg pickup_vehicle_1 leg_1)
(activate_delivery_vehicle_on_leg delivery_vehicle_1 leg_1)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(assign_time_window_to_shipment pickup_vehicle_1 time_window_slot_1 mode_road)
(reconcile_and_release_pickup_vehicle_asset pickup_vehicle_1 vehicle_asset_a time_window_slot_1)
(assign_transport_mode_to_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 mode_road)
(assign_time_window_to_shipment delivery_vehicle_1 time_window_slot_1 mode_road)
(reconcile_and_release_delivery_vehicle_asset delivery_vehicle_1 vehicle_asset_b time_window_slot_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning scenario in PDDL for vehicle capacity constrained load planning. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Examining PDDL planning scenario  
- The task focuses on vehicle capacity-constrained load planning using PDDL.  
- The output must be a sequence of ground PDDL actions, each on a new line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).

## Examining initial state  
- Objects include pickup_vehicle_1, delivery_vehicle_1, vehicle_unit_truck_1, vehicle_unit_truck_2, and others like vehicle_asset_a,b,c, mode_road, and cargo_pallet_1.  
- Initial facts show assignments like pickup_vehicle_1 linked to origin_hub_a, delivery_vehicle_1 to destination_hub_b, and both trucks assigned to leg_1.  

## Analyzing types and relationships  
- Pickup_vehicle and delivery_vehicle are subtypes of vehicle_role_group, which is under shipment; vehicle_unit is a subtype of fleet_unit_group, also under shipment.  
- This means pickup_vehicle_1 and delivery_vehicle_1 are treated as shipments, impacting how planning actions are structured.  

## Reviewing goal conditions  
- The goal is to achieve returned_to_pool for pickup_vehicle_1, delivery_vehicle_1, vehicle_unit_truck_1, and vehicle_unit_truck_2.  
- Achieving this requires entities to be plan_entity_scheduled, committed_for_dispatch, and assigned a time_window, possibly via actions like reconcile_and_release_pickup_vehicle_asset.

## Analyzing planning steps  
- To create a transport leg, need pickup_origin_confirmed and delivery_destination_confirmed for pickup_vehicle_1 and delivery_vehicle_1, plus reserved origin and destination nodes.  
- Actions like reserve_origin_node_for_pickup require pickup_vehicle_1 to be plan_entity_ready, assigned a transport mode, and linked to origin_hub_a, but not yet reserved or staged.  

## Checking readiness conditions  
- Making pickup_vehicle_1 and delivery_vehicle_1 ready involves registering them, assigning assets, and ensuring a transport mode, per mark_shipment_ready preconditions.  
- Vehicle units (truck_1, truck_2) also need readiness for staging, suggesting they share similar registration and asset assignment steps.  

## Exploring asset allocation  
- Three vehicle assets (a, b, c) are available, but four entities need assignment, hinting at potential sequential sharing or additional assets.  
- Plan begins with registering all four shipments (pickup_vehicle_1, delivery_vehicle_1, vehicle_unit_truck_1, vehicle_unit_truck_2) to enable further actions.

## Exploring resource constraints  
- Only three vehicle assets (a, b, c) are available, but four entities (pickup1, delivery1, truck1, truck2) need assignment, suggesting sequential processing.  
- Transport mode (mode_road) and dock are single resources, requiring assignment, use, and release (e.g., unassign_transport_mode_from_shipment) to reuse for multiple entities.  

## Managing asset availability  
- Vehicle assets lack an unassign action; they must remain assigned until released via reconcile after scheduling, limiting reuse.  
- To process multiple entities, fully dispatch, schedule, and release one before assigning assets to the next, ensuring resource availability.  

## Handling leg creation  
- Creating a transport leg (leg_1) requires both pickup_vehicle_1 and delivery_vehicle_1 to be ready simultaneously, each with assigned resources like mode and asset.  
- Assigning the same mode to both at once isn't possible due to availability constraints, so plan involves assigning to one, marking ready, then unassigning to reuse for the other.

## Managing cargo and dock resources  
- Cargo_pallet_1 is consumed during pickup staging, making it unavailable for delivery, so delivery uses reserve and confirm preparation instead.  
- The single loading dock requires careful sequencing; delivery needs to be marked ready before allocating the dock, ensuring availability.  

## Sequencing shipment steps  
- For pickup_vehicle_1, register, assign assets, and stage cargo at origin, but unassigning the transport mode keeps it ready without the mode.  
- For delivery_vehicle_1, register, assign assets, mark ready, allocate the dock, reserve destination, then confirm preparation, setting it ready for leg creation.  

## Addressing leg readiness issues  
- Creating the transport leg requires both vehicles ready, but finalizing leg readiness needs pickup_vehicle_1 to have an assigned transport mode, which was unassigned earlier.  
- This creates a conflict, as the current approach unassigns the mode to reuse it, but leg readiness actions still require it assigned.

## Assigning Handling Equipment  
- After vehicle staging is confirmed, assign handling equipment (e.g., forklift_1) to the vehicle unit, ensuring it’s ready and the equipment is available.  
- This step sets the equipment as assigned and unavailable, preparing for further vehicle preparation actions.  

## Managing Dock Allocation  
- Release the loading dock from delivery_vehicle_1 once preparation is complete to make it available for the next vehicle unit.  
- Allocate the dock to vehicle_unit_truck_1 after staging, ensuring it’s ready and the dock is available, for subsequent preparation steps.  

## Preparing Vehicle with Driver  
- Assign a driver (e.g., driver_1) to vehicle_unit_truck_1 after handling equipment is set, ensuring the driver is available and the vehicle is ready.  
- Use assign_driver_and_prepare_vehicle_with_load_profile to prepare the vehicle, confirming crew assignment and readiness for load profile, given origin handling requirements.

## Managing Dock Availability  
- Release the loading dock from delivery_vehicle_1 to make it available for truck1, ensuring smooth sequencing.  
- Dock is now free, allowing truck1 to proceed with dock allocation in subsequent steps.  

## Registering and Preparing Truck1  
- Register truck1, assign mode_road, and mark it ready, ensuring all prerequisites are met for staging.  
- Stage container_1 for leg_1, reserving a service window for truck1 to handle origin requirements, confirming staging and service application.  

## Continuing Vehicle Preparation  
- Assign handling equipment (forklift_1) to truck1 and allocate the loading dock, preparing for vehicle readiness.  
- Assign driver_1 to truck1 and use load profile preparation, ensuring crew assignment and readiness for load profile, given origin handling needs.  

## Finalizing Dispatch Readiness  
- Commit truck1 to loading with pickup_vehicle_1 and delivery_vehicle_1, setting vehicle_loading_committed after ensuring all readiness conditions.  
- Finalize dispatch for truck1 by setting vehicle_ready_for_dispatch, enabling scheduling with a time window slot.

## Continuing Vehicle Preparation  
- Assign transport mode (mode_road) to pickup_vehicle_1 to finalize staging and leg preparation, ensuring mode availability after previous unassignments.  
- Create transport leg with pickup_vehicle_1 and delivery_vehicle_1, connecting origin_hub_a to destination_hub_b, confirming origin and destination readiness.  

## Finalizing Dispatch Readiness  
- Finalize origin staging for pickup_vehicle_1, marking it ready and reserving the origin node, while keeping origin_node_staged status true.  
- Finalize transport leg readiness for leg_1 with pickup_vehicle_1 and mode_road, ensuring all preconditions for dispatch are met.

## Preparing Truck2 for Dispatch
- Assign forklift_1 and loading_dock_1 to vehicle_unit_truck_2, preparing for container_2 and leg_1 operations.
- Assign driver_1 to vehicle_unit_truck_2 and prepare with load_profile_fragile, ensuring crew and load readiness.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
